FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Klarna pursues U.S. bank charter as fintech strategy shifts; regional bank M&A continues while Fed tightens screws on undercapitalized lenders.

bullets.text: Klarna applies for ILC charter with FDIC, signaling major pivot from BNPL to full-service banking—deposit-taking and lending under U.S. regulation.
Regions Financial acquires Frazer Lanier to bolster municipal bond underwriting—fitting stated M&A strategy, deal size undisclosed.
Fed hits Small Business Bank (Kansas) with third enforcement action since 2023 for severe undercapitalization; 30-day remediation deadline.
Bank7 pursues 71% stake in Century Bank after largest shareholder defaults, forcing collateralized share sale.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Wealth management consolidation accelerates across TAMPs, retirement platforms, and RIA roll-ups, though some founders are betting against the trend.

bullets.text: Vestmark's TAMP platform hits $50B AUM (5x growth) after launching standalone UMA product with tax-loss harvesting; major asset managers (T. Rowe Price, BlackRock, Capital Group, VanEck) now refer clients directly.
Empower acquires Milliman for $340M to add 1.53M retirement plan participants and cross-sell wealth services into workplace benefits platform.
Aspen Standard buys undisclosed Michigan RIA for $1.3B, bringing its eight-affiliate platform to $15B AUM; holding-company consolidation continues steady pace.
Integrated Partners counters consolidation wave by recruiting $4B AUM this year as PE-free, founder-owned alternative emphasizing advisor autonomy.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Catastrophe bonds surge to record $11.3B in Q2 as climate risks reshape underwriting; M&A accelerates with Sompo-Service and Duck Creek-Send deals.

bullets.text: Cat bond issuance hit $11.3B in Q2—a quarterly record across 48 transactions—signaling robust demand for climate risk transfer.
Sompo International acquiring Service Insurance Companies in all-cash deal to expand US workers' comp footprint, extending consolidation in specialized lines.
Duck Creek Technologies acquiring Send Technology Solutions to embed agentic AI across P&C underwriting—automation becoming table stakes in ops.
EU banking authority developing heat-risk metrics for lenders while wildfire acceleration strains insurer evacuation liability in California.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: 4 stories spanning BNPL expansion, big bank payments M&A, fintech cross-border consolidation, and emerging lending partnerships.

bullets.text: Klarna applies for US banking license, signaling major pivot from BNPL into regulated banking services.
JPMorgan and BofA in talks to acquire Fiserv's debit network; deal could reshape payments infrastructure but no valuation yet.
LemFi (Nigeria fintech) clears UK regulatory approval to acquire Wealth8, expanding into wealth management for diaspora clients.
Ex-Runway Growth CIO Greg Greifeld launches new consumer lending business; SIMCo and PIMCO announce infrastructure debt partnership.

PE & Deals.lede: Institutional LPs staying committed despite headwinds: GPIF grows PE 26%, Eurazeo's secondaries fund doubles, while discipline replaces easy-money returns.

bullets.text: GPIF added $1.4bn to PE portfolio (26% YoY growth) despite 'challenging environment'—signals Japan's $1.8trn pension giant maintaining conviction in asset class.
StepStone CEO: PE model survives post-easy-money era, but uniform returns myth is dead; winners now separated by discipline and selectivity.
Eurazeo closed $2.3bn secondaries fund (2.3x Fund IV), exceeding target—strong LP appetite for secondaries as dry powder redeploys.
Chinese PE repositioning post-regulatory squeeze; more disciplined market emerging for committed LPs despite years of capital flight.

Markets & Macro.lede: Credit markets tightening while regulators ease rules; structural shifts in money markets and pension surpluses reshaping capital deployment.

bullets.text: HSBC pulling back from private credit renewals signals risk appetite cooling in $1.2T+ market—watch for contagion across other lenders.
OIS spread collapse ends 22-year bank funding arbitrage, reflecting structural Fed policy shifts and tighter money market mechanics post-2020.
Bank of England easing countercyclical capital buffers to unlock lending capacity—counterpoint to HSBC's de-risking, suggesting policy/market tension.
UK DB pensions now in surplus for first time in years, opening $100B+ debate on contribution holidays vs. benefit enhancements vs. sponsor releases.

Newsletters.lede: 5 stories spanning crypto strategy reversals, regulatory headwinds for novel ETFs, tax planning tailwinds, wealth concentration, and AI company IPO momentum.

bullets.text: MicroStrategy's bitcoin holdings down 52% from $126K peak to below $60K, forcing a reckoning on the 'never sell' thesis that defined the strategy.
SEC turning up heat on novel ETFs including leveraged products—potential regulatory friction ahead for emerging product launches.
IRS reclassified 'Trump Account' contributions as current-interest gifts, expanding tax-advantaged universe for HNW clients and lowering gift-tax friction.
Wealth concentration accelerating across income tiers per UBS report, driving advisor hiring and AUM growth in wealth management.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: Four stories on market structure and incentives: government equity stakes distort tech competition, labor income is being reclassified as capital gains, prediction markets show real-time signal aggregation, and automation's accuracy-versus-experience tradeoff.

bullets.text: Government equity holdings in tech firms (e.g., Deutsche Telekom) misalign incentives and suppress innovation—a structural headwind for competitive dynamism.
Labor's income share decline may partly reflect tax classification shifts: stock and pass-through compensation counted as capital gains rather than wages, masking true compensation forms.
Prediction markets moved Maine Democratic dropout odds from 9% to 96% overnight on scandal news—demonstrating rapid information aggregation that could reshape institutional decision-making.

all.lede: Vestmark's TAMP platform hits $50B AUM on standalone RIA momentum, while Klarna pursues U.S. banking expansion and JPMorgan/BofA eye Fiserv's debit network.

bullets.text: Vestmark's TAMP AUM surges past $50B (5x growth) after launching standalone retail UMA with tax-loss harvesting; T. Rowe Price, BlackRock, Capital Group, VanEck now refer clients directly.
Klarna applies for U.S. ILC charter with FDIC, signaling major strategic pivot from buy-now-pay-later into regulated deposit-taking and lending.
JPMorgan and BofA in acquisition talks for Fiserv's debit payments network; no valuation or timeline disclosed yet.
